name: Mage (Go-based Build Tool)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); pure Go make alternative

auth.notes: Local build tool; no auth required

performance.notes: Compiled Go functions; fast execution; targets are cached

agent_readiness.notes: Make alternative using pure Go. Write build targets as Go functions in magefile.go. Functions are auto-discovered as targets. Supports namespaces, dependencies between targets, context cancellation. Full Go type safety and IDE support. No shell scripting — pure Go logic. Works cross-platform without bash. Alternative to Makefile, Taskfile, Just for Go projects. Used widely in Go ecosystem (e.g., Kubernetes tooling).

gotchas: Targets must be exported Go functions (capitalized)
Compilation step adds startup overhead vs Makefile
Go module setup required even for simple projects
best_when: Go projects wanting type-safe, cross-platform build scripts
Teams that prefer Go over shell/YAML for build logic
Complex build pipelines needing Go's full expressiveness
Projects already using Go where shell scripting is awkward
avoid_when: Simple 2-3 command builds (Makefile or task is simpler)
summary: Mage is a Make alternative for Go projects using pure Go functions as build targets. Write type-safe, cross-platform build logic in Go instead of shell scripts or YAML. Functions are auto-discovered as targets, support dependencies and namespaces. Full Go toolchain support with IDE completion. Used in Kubernetes ecosystem and complex Go projects requiring build logic that benefits from Go's expressiveness.
